text:
CT CHEST, ABDOMEN, AND PELVIS WITH IV CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: 59-year-old male with known metastatic sigmoid colon adenocarcinoma (KRAS G12D mutant, pMMR/MSS), currently on second-line therapy with FOLFIRI plus Cyramza. Prior disease progression on maintenance 5-FU/bev with hepatic progression and new bilateral pulmonary nodules identified at prior scan approximately 14 months ago from initial staging. Presenting for response assessment after approximately 4 months on current second-line regimen.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis performed with IV contrast (Omnipaque 350, 120 mL) administered via power injector. Portal venous phase acquisitions through the abdomen and pelvis; venous phase through the chest. Oral contrast administered. Coronal and sagittal reconstructions obtained and reviewed. Compared with prior CT CAP from approximately 6 weeks prior and earlier studies.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and airways: The previously identified bilateral pulmonary nodules demonstrate interval improvement when compared to the prior examination.
- Right lower lobe nodule: Solid, well-circumscribed, measures approximately 0.7 x 0.6 cm (previously 0.8 x 0.7 cm). Mild but perceptible decrease in size.
- Left upper lobe nodule: Measures approximately 0.5 x 0.5 cm, unchanged from prior exam.
- Right middle lobe nodule: Measures approximately 0.4 x 0.3 cm, slightly smaller than prior (previously 0.5 x 0.4 cm).
No new pulmonary nodules identified. No consolidation, pleural effusion, or pneumothorax. No endobronchial lesions. Airways patent.

Mediastinum and hila: No mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy. No pericardial effusion. Heart size within normal limits. Aorta and great vessels unremarkable in caliber.

Chest wall and soft tissues: Subcutaneous port-a-cath device overlying the right anterior chest wall in stable position, unchanged. No evidence of port-related complication.

ABDOMEN:

Liver: The liver is normal in size. Parenchymal attenuation is mildly heterogeneous, consistent with background treatment-related changes.
- Segment VI (right lobe) lesion: Hypoenhancing lesion measuring approximately 1.8 x 1.5 cm (previously 2.1 x 1.8 cm at prior progression scan). This dominant hepatic lesion has decreased appreciably from its largest measurement at time of disease progression and is now approaching the nadir size seen at end of induction chemotherapy. Enhancement pattern shows peripheral hypoenhancement with partial rim-like enhancement at arterial phase, consistent with treated metastatic deposit.
- Segment IVb lesion: Faintly perceptible hypoenhancing focus measuring approximately 0.8 x 0.7 cm (previously 1.0 x 0.9 cm). Decreased in size from prior progression scan.
- Segment II (left lobe) lesion: Tiny hypoenhancing focus, approximately 0.5 x 0.4 cm, essentially stable to mildly decreased. Difficult to characterize with confidence at this size; morphologically consistent with persistent treated deposit.
No new hepatic lesions identified. No biliary ductal dilatation. Portal vein patent with normal hepatopetal flow on portal venous phase.

Biliary: Gallbladder present, no cholelithiasis, no pericholecystic inflammatory change. Common bile duct not dilated.

Pancreas: Normal size and attenuation. Pancreatic duct not dilated. No peripancreatic stranding or fluid.

Spleen: Normal size and homogeneous attenuation. No focal splenic lesion.

Adrenal glands: Bilateral adrenal glands unremarkable. No adrenal mass or nodule.

Kidneys: Both kidneys are normal in size with smooth cortical contours and symmetric nephrogram. No hydronephrosis. No renal masses. No nephrolithiasis.

Gastrointestinal tract and mesentery: Sigmoid colon: Circumferential mural thickening with mucosal enhancement identified in the sigmoid at the site of the known primary tumor, measuring approximately 2.7 x 2.3 cm in cross-sectional dimension on axial images (minimally changed from prior, previously 2.7 x 2.3 cm). The degree of mural thickening appears stable. No associated bowel obstruction. No perforation. No adjacent abscess or fistula. Pericolonic fat stranding is mild and not significantly changed from prior. Previously enlarged pericolonic lymph nodes remain undetectable on this examination.
Remainder of the large and small bowel without obstruction, perforation, or focal abnormality.

Peritoneum and retroperitoneum: No ascites. No peritoneal nodularity or omental caking. No retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy. No free fluid.

Vasculature: Aorta and inferior vena cava patent and normal in caliber. No vascular occlusion.

PELVIS:

Bladder: Normal. No mural thickening or intraluminal mass.

Prostate and seminal vesicles: Prostate mildly enlarged, consistent with BPH, stable.

Rectum and pelvic structures: No pelvic lymphadenopathy. No free pelvic fluid. Sigmoid and rectosigmoid junction as described above. No new pelvic masses.

BONES AND SOFT TISSUES:

Visualized osseous structures: No suspicious lytic or sclerotic lesions identified. No cortical destruction. No new bone lesion to suggest osseous metastatic disease. Mild multilevel degenerative change of the lumbar spine, chronic appearing.

Soft tissues: No abnormal soft tissue masses. Subcutaneous and body wall tissues unremarkable.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Bilateral pulmonary metastases: The three previously identified bilateral pulmonary nodules (right lower lobe, left upper lobe, right middle lobe) are collectively stable to mildly decreased in size compared to the prior progression scan. The right lower lobe and right middle lobe nodules appear very slightly smaller. No new pulmonary nodules identified.

2. Hepatic metastases: All three visualized hepatic lesions demonstrate interval reduction from their measurements at time of disease progression. The dominant Segment VI lesion has decreased from 2.1 x 1.8 cm to 1.8 x 1.5 cm. Segment IVb lesion similarly decreased from 1.0 x 0.9 cm to 0.8 x 0.7 cm. Segment II lesion essentially stable at 0.5 x 0.4 cm. Overall, the hepatic disease appears favorably responsive to current therapy.

3. Primary sigmoid colon mass: Stable in size at approximately 2.7 x 2.3 cm. No obstructive or complicating features.

4. No new sites of metastatic disease identified. No peritoneal carcinomatosis. No osseous metastases.

5. Port-a-cath in stable position without complication.

Overall, there is an encouraging response to second-line FOLFIRI plus ramu, with hepatic lesions showing meaningful interval reduction from prior progression measurements and pulmonary nodules stable to slightly smaller. Disease appears to be responding to current treatment.

Reporting Radiologist: [Attending Radiologist, Body Imaging Division]
Clinical Correlation: Recommended as clinically indicated.
